title: abies excelsa var. baldensis
intro: Abies excelsa var. baldensis, also known as the Fraser fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of Italy, specifically in the Apennine Mountains. The Fraser fir has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Abies excelsa var. baldensis include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from muscle and joint pain. Herbal preparations of the Fraser fir typically include essential oils extracted from the leaves and twigs, which can be used in aromatherapy, teas, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
